programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: More of Jeff Wright's close co-workers and friends testified for the prosecution's case against his wife accused of killing him. They gave a different viewpoint on the couple's marriage in the lead up to the fatal stabbing at home. Next, one of the defendant's close friends and next-door neighbor took the stand. Specific questions of domestic violence was asked. This was the Defense's claim of why Susan killed her husband in a fatal knife attack.

programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: Jurors listened to a distraught close friend who was lied to about Jeff Wright's whereabouts and eventual death. Prosecutors wanted to convince the jury that if Susan could lie first, to close friends and family members then later to police - how could you believe her now? These neighbors and close friends testified that they saw nothing out of the ordinary in the couple's marriage. This was different that the Defense's view where Jeff was this habitual drug abuser who was violent to his wife.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.desc: "Unrighteous Act" follows the investigation into the brutal murder of Karen Klaas and how, 41 years later, cutting-edge DNA forensics solved her murder.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1976, 16-year-old Pamela Maurer leaves a friend's house to walk to a nearby location for a soft drink. She never makes it home. The next morning, road workers discover her body along College Road. New DNA technology solves the case in 2019.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1992, the decapitated body of a young woman is discovered near the Arizona Canal in Phoenix. Ten months later, another body - this time a teen - is located nearby. Dedicated detectives use forensic science to catch the infamous killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1979, 16-year-old Kim Bryant disappears from outside a Dairy Queen in Las Vegas. Three weeks later, her body is found: she had been sexually assaulted and bludgeoned to death. 40 years later, genetic genealogy finally names the killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives

programme.desc: After a parole officer was found shot dead in his car, the FBI, DEA and NYPD struggled to contain the mounting violence from drug gangs that continued to escalate. Authorities sought to put two ruthless drug kingpins behind bars for the crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the 1970s, a Croatian terrorist group committed acts against non-supportive Croatians in the United States.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1998, a wealthy woman disappeared from her home in Jackson, Mississippi. The only clue was a mysterious ransom note listing twelve names but no specific instructions. The FBI struggled to find answers in the cryptic message.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A young mother and her two daughters were sexually assaulted and brutally murdered in Anchorage.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A class field trip ended with an 11-year-old girl missing. When the parents failed to locate their daughter in their quiet Illinois town, the FBI joined the investigation, working with friends and family in an urgent search to find the missing girl.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1999, a brutal serial killer who traveled by train terrorized Texas. The FBI assembled a special task force to profile the violent drifter and help locate him.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: While on trial for rape, 33-year-old Brian Nichols goes on a violent rampage that results in three murders.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Denver Simmons is as dangerous as he is ruthless. Incarcerated for murdering a woman and her 14-year-old son, his violence only spiraled further once he was behind bars.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Born into a notorious crime family, robber-turned-killer Erica Jenkins goes on a murder spree in Omaha.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Remorseless murderer Casey Pigge's main ambition is to be the ultimate serial killer. If unrestrained, he remains a deadly threat.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Nothing was going to stop Ricky Dubose from breaking free of prison. Whilst being transported on a bus between facilities, he killed two correctional officers before escaping - and going on the run.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Behind a polite, intelligent exterior, Michael hid violent obsessions. He attacked young women whenever the urge struck, becoming one of Connecticut's most prolific killers until investigators finally stopped him.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Two criminals, who met in prison while serving time for previous murders, struck up a friendship, which had deadly consequences.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Catherine Nevin and her husband Tom were successful publicans and property developers in the Dublin and Wicklow areas. Catherine reported a break-in and violent attack at their pub one night in 1996, which left Tom dead.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Carla, a bright and beloved teen, was abducted and murdered, shattering her community. For decades, the case remained unsolved until new investigative efforts finally pointed to Glen as the man responsible.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A violent spree left three innocent people dead as Anthony robbed, fled and attacked without hesitation. Police launched a massive manhunt, racing to catch him before he could claim yet another victim.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: He portrayed himself as a pillar of the community, but the story of this cult killer was deeply sinister.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A man is jailed for the murder of an author, which leads to a second conviction for the death of his wife.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Wayne Adam Ford is an American serial killer who strangled and dismembered three of his four victims. The first body was found in October 1997 and had been so thoroughly dismembered that her identity remains unknown.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Over a 25-year career of murder, Richard Biegenwald killed at least six people, but he is suspected of many more murders. Imprisoned in 1958 aged just 18 for shooting dead a grocery store owner, Biegenwald was released 17 years later.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: When Mary Gough was found dead at the bottom of her stairs in her family home, her husband Colin Whelan appeared shocked. But a postmortem revealed her injuries weren't consistent with a fall. Whelan had murdered her for an insurance payout.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An investigation into the man known as the 'Babes in the Wood Killer,' who evaded justice for thirty years.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Douglas Clark and Carol Bundy were a deadly duo who terrorised the streets of LA in the summer of 1980.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Having committed over 27 assaults on correctional officers, Billy Tracy is one of the most violent offenders on death row.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Imprisoned after a botched robbery that ends with the fatal shooting of an innocent man, Craig Wissink later kills a corrections officer with a fire extinguisher in a brutal attack.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Paul Gruber's daughter suspected something was wrong when she received birthday cards addressed from her father, but written in a stranger's handwriting.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2004, Mary Ann Clibbery was found brutally murdered in her Illinois business and investigators had to determine if this was a robbery gone wrong or a calculated murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a hit-and-run accident claimed the life of a high school athlete, everyone in town mourned his passing. Finding the killer was a long shot at best.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In a quiet village in Great Britain, a farmer came upon a chilling sight. Impaled on his fence post was a severed lamb's head along with a note which read, "You next."

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, a 12-year-old was found murdered in her home in Minnesota. Despite a meticulous search of the home and an exhaustive investigation, police had no suspects.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1992, 22-year-old Dawn Bruce is brutally murdered in her Virginia apartment. Investigators notice a blood smear on a pillowcase that appears to have been made by one of the killer's fingers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a human skull retrieved from an Ohio pond reveals a ghastly crime. Markings on the skull indicate that the victim had been stabbed multiple times and that the teeth had been removed with needle-nose pliers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a talented television news anchor is shot to death outside her home, it appears to be a crime of passion perpetrated by an obsessed fan.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Just weeks before a witness is to testify against the man accused of sexually assaulting her, she is murdered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, Susan Fassett was gunned down as she left her church choir practice in Poughkeepsie, NY. After clearing her husband, police turned their attention to Fred Andros, with whom Susan had an affair.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Janice Johnson was found dead at the foot of her stairs. Police in Nova Scotia had to answer the question: Was it an accident or murder?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After the murder of Seattle residents, evidence from the couple's dog eventually ties a murderer to the scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1986, Gary Dale Larson was stabbed to death in his Edmond, Oklahoma home and then the killer sexually assaulted Larson's girlfriend Janet Haynes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1995, the founder of American Atheists disappeared with one of her sons, her granddaughter and $500,000 in gold coins. The police investigated several suspects, including David R. Waters, who worked for American Atheists.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Edward Honaker was sentenced to three life terms in prison, but he steadfastly maintained his innocence.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 29-year-old woman was killed instantly when a bomb exploded in her home. The device was so powerful that shrapnel was embedded in houses across the street. The bomber had not only knowledge and skill, but also a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Tracey Frame, 35, was convicted of killing David Nixon in April 2002 after he planned to end their relationship.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Lisa Manderach and her daughter Devon left their Pennsylvania home to go shopping but didn't return home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: With no forensic evidence inside a murder scene, investigators were baffled. But they suspected that the victim's dog had witnessed the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For more than a year, hateful letters were sent to a school teacher in Pennsylvania. DNA analysis would eventually help seal the perpetrator's fate.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A family break turns into a nightmare when the wife is found face-down in a lake; with no idea whether it was murder, an accident or suicide, police turn to a new forensic technique.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial rapist is on the loose in a Texas town. No one knew where he'd strike next, but the audacity of his attacks and the escalation of violence against his victims made finding him imperative.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three homicides on two continents looked like professional executions. Investigators on both sides of the Atlantic needed to find out if they were related and, if they were, who or what they had in common.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was brutally murdered in her home and the only witnesses to the crime were the family dogs. An expert in canine behavior was convinced the killer knew both the victim and the animals.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1987, a human skull and some bones were discovered at a boy scout camp near Farmington, Missouri. A facial reconstruction aided the investigators in this case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim had been sexually assaulted and stabbed to death on the beach. A pair of men's tennis shoes was discovered near her body.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
